zh.lifeSummary: 當Aleonore Beauthamp於1274年出生在英國薩默塞特時，她的父親John de Beauchamp11271歲，母親Cecily de Vivonne17歲。她於1294年與Hugh Fleury在英國薩默塞特梅利特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aleonore Beauthamp于1274年出生在英国薩默塞特时，她的父亲John de Beauchamp11271岁，母亲Cecily de Vivonne17岁。她于1294年与Hugh Fleury在英国薩默塞特梅利特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Beauthamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Aleonore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Aleonore Beauthamp was born in 1274, in Somerset, England, her father, John de Beauchamp, was 11271 and her mother, Cecily de Vivonne, was 17. She married Hugh Fleury in 1294, in Merriott, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aleonore Beauthamp est née en 1274, à Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John de Beauchamp, avait 11271 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Vivonne, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh Fleury en 1294, à Merriott, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aleonore Beauthamp 1274 in Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Beauchamp 11271 und ihre Mutter Cecily de Vivonne 17. Sie hat 1294 in Merriott, Somerset, England Hugh Fleury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aleonore Beauthamp è nata il nel 1274 nel Somerset, England, sua padre, John de Beauchamp, aveva 11271 anni e sua madre, Cecily de Vivonne, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Hugh Fleury nel 1294, nel Merriott, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Merriott, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトメリオット
ja.lifeSummary: Aleonore Beauthampが1274年にイギリスソメルセトで生まれた時，父John de Beauchampは11271歳，母Cecily de Vivonneは17歳であった。1294年にイギリスソメルセトメリオットでHugh Fleuryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Aleonore Beauthamp(이)가 1274년 영국 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Beauchamp(은)는 11271세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Vivonne(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1294년 영국 서머세트 머리엇에서 Hugh Fleury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aleonore Beauthamp nasceu em 1274, em Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Beauchamp, tinha 11271 anos e sua mãe, Cecily de Vivonne, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh Fleury em 1294, em Merriott, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aleonore Beauthamp родилась в 1274 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Beauchamp было 11271 год, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Vivonne, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Fleury в 1294 г. в местности мирриотт, сомирсет, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Aleonore Beauthamp nació en 1274, en Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Beauchamp, tenía 11271 años y su mamá, Cecily de Vivonne, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Hugh Fleury en 1294, en Merriott, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
